generalRequirements.note: LAP must usually be valid for 3 months. LAP is not applicable for professionals, household employees, religious bodies, and sports sector cases. Those use a letter of accommodation instead.

generalRequirements.item: CSG receipt + list of local workers

Last 3 years. Financial statement for sport sector. Not applicable for jockeys, household employees and religious bodies.

generalRequirements.item: Letter of Authorisation + NIC of registered user

generalRequirements.note: Commercial partnerships or 'Societe' also need a Certificate of Posting. Household employees: not applicable.
If applicable: agency letter, director NIC, recruitment licence. Not needed for renewal.
Passport should have 6 months validity for new applications.
6 months validity. Not applicable for renewal.

generalRequirements.item: Vetted contract of employment or salary proof
generalRequirements.note: If salary is 50,000 MUR or below, contract vetting applies. If above 50,000 MUR, employer documentary evidence of salary is required.

notes: HEC recognition is required for academic qualifications before work permit application.
If the qualification is technical/vocational and not covered by HEC, recognition must be sought from MQA.

Professionals may also be eligible for an Occupation Permit under the Immigration Act.

requirements: Valid Freeport Certificate from EDB Mauritius

notes: Licence and TEWF receipt should have 3 months validity.
If no vocational qualification, at least 2 years work experience in the grade applied for is required.
English and/or French literacy is mandatory for specific grades.
Most of these are N/A for renewal.

callQuestions: Who is the speaker / contact person?
What is the outcome: no answer, not interested, or interested?
If interested, how many expatriates are needed? Example: 2 to 4 expatriates
For which post / grade are the expatriates needed?
What nationality of expatriates is required?
How many local workers does the company currently have?
How many expatriates does the company currently have?
Does the company already have LAP?
If LAP exists, what is the total capacity and how many places remain available?
If there is no lodging, note that housing search is needed near the company location.
For construction companies only: does the company have a CIA certificate?
Does the company already have an EDB account and Mauritius Jobs account?
For candidate documents, are passports valid, passport photos ready, and medical tests available?
If the client is very interested, flag for Mr. Vayid to follow up directly.

workflow.details: Record speaker name, date, outcome, expatriate need, post / grade, nationality, local workers, current expatriates, LAP status, and company location.
For construction companies only, confirm whether the CIA certificate is available.
If LAP already exists, verify capacity and remaining places. If there is no lodging, start housing search near the company.
Mr. Vayid handles lodging visit while fire and sanitary steps move in parallel.
Confirm grade / post and number needed, then request more CVs than the exact need for better selection.
Record agencies, CVs, and candidate status in Excel and share the updated sheet with the team after screening.
Check client availability, confirm WhatsApp or Teams, and schedule interviews one by one if there are many candidates.
Before interview, email CVs and a short summary of each expatriate profile to the client.
After interview, ask the client which expatriate is chosen, then send confirmation email and team update.
Where required, proceed with vacancy form, Director ID, email follow-up, and team-verified EIC report.
Check passport, passport validity, passport photo, medical tests, and save documents in one folder per candidate.
Medical covering letter is deposited at Ebène. Fire is handled in Port Louis and sanitary depends on region.
Submit LAP set after supporting clearances and track the applicable fees and status at Victoria House, Port Louis.
Prepare COE / contract, apply through EDB, handle VET for Madagascar only, and track work permit approval.
Liaise with PIO for entry permit and with the agent for flight tickets.
After the expatriate arrives in Mauritius, follow up with PIO for residence permit and payment / validity checks.

documents: Health Clearance from Ministry of Health and Wellness / Quality of Life
Site plan and layout plan of employees' lodging accommodation
Owner authorisation + owner ID copy or lease agreement
Applicant ID copy if someone other than director is handling application
Director authorisation letter allowing the person to submit/amend the application
lap.renewal: Apply within 3 months before expiry, with fresh health and fire clearances. Permit lapses 30 days after expiry if not renewed. Late renewal may attract a 50% surcharge under the regulations.

rules: No employer may use a building as lodging accommodation without a permit.
Permit validity is up to 36 months.
Permit must be exhibited in a conspicuous place in the lodging accommodation.
Accommodation should not be in the same building as the factory or place of work, subject to transitional provisions.
Each room must have an updated lodger inventory sheet.
Bedroom floor area must be at least 4.6 m² for one employee with single bed, and 5.2 m² for any 2 employees with bunk beds.
Beds must be separate with proper mattresses. Minimum spacing is 1.1 m between single beds and 1.2 m where bunk beds are used.
Separate lodging accommodation areas must be provided for male and female employees.
Bedroom height must be at least 2.5 m.
Ventilation openings must be at least one tenth of floor area, with sufficient natural or artificial lighting.
Sanitary facilities must be accessible at all times, supplied with clean running water, and cleaned/disinfected every day.
Bathrooms and toilets need impervious non-skid floors; sanitary walls tiled to at least 2 m; bathroom walls tiled or waterproof-painted to at least 2 m.
Water storage tanks should be cleaned and disinfected at least every 6 months.
Kitchen must have adequate cooking facilities, refrigeration, air extraction, screened openings, grease trap drainage, separate scullery, and clean dining area.
Fire rules include emergency exits / escape provisions, extinguishers, warning system where applicable, and no smoking or cooking in bedrooms.
At least two socket outlets should be provided in each bedroom per lodger and no multiplug should be used in bedrooms.
A first-aid box and enough appointed persons / first-aiders should be available, with transport arrangements to hospital if needed.

waterClosets.overRule: For over 150, provide 1 additional sanitary convenience for every 25 male and for every 25 female employees.

washBasins.overRule: For over 125, provide 1 additional wash-basin for every 20 male or every 20 female employees.

bathrooms.overRule: For over 55, provide 1 additional bathroom for every 15 male or every 15 female employees.

remarksExamples.format: DD/MM/YYYY - Interested - Needs [number] expatriates for [post/grade] - Nationality: [x]
DD/MM/YYYY - No lodging available - Housing search needed near company

firstCallChecklist.text: Record the name of the speaker and the date of call.
If there is no answer, write the date first in Remarks / Comments, then note no answer.
If the company is not interested, write the date first in Remarks / Comments, then note not interested.
If the company is interested, note the number of expatriates needed, the post / grade, and the nationality required.
Irrespective of sector, ask how many local workers and how many expatriates the company currently has.
If the company already has expatriates, record the current number already employed.
Ask whether the company already has LAP and how many places remain available under the current permit.
If there is no lodging, note that housing search will be required near the company location.
For construction companies only, ask whether the CIA certificate is available.
If the prospect is very interested, flag the case for Mr. Vayid to follow up directly.

interestedClientWorkflow: Confirm exact manpower need by post / grade and nationality.
Record current local workers, current expatriates, and LAP status.
If LAP exists, verify total capacity, already occupied places, and remaining capacity.
If there is no lodging, search housing near the company location.
Lodging search, fire, and sanitary steps move in parallel once the case becomes active.
Very interested cases are escalated for direct follow-up by Mr. Vayid.
candidateSourcing: Confirm the grade / post and number of expatriates needed before contacting agencies.
Use the existing email templates when contacting agencies.
Request more CVs than the exact need so the client has better choice.
Example: if 5 expatriates are needed, request around 10 CVs for better evaluation.
Track agency name, candidate name, and CV status in the database / Excel.
After screening, send the updated candidate Excel to the team.
interviewProcess: Ask the client whether they are available on certain dates for interviews.
Confirm whether the interview will be on WhatsApp or Teams.
If there are many candidates, schedule them one by one.
Before interview, send the client an email with CVs and a short summary of each expatriate profile.
Schedule interview with the client, expatriate, and yourself.
Create a WhatsApp group if needed, then remove candidates one by one after interview is completed.
After interview, email the client to ask which expatriate(s) they choose.
Send confirmation email and report the outcome to the team.
Prepare / research standard questions to ask expatriates during interview.
eicProcess: When potential expatriate candidates are confirmed, proceed with EIC where required.
Tell EIC that suitable candidates were not obtained in Mauritius and collect the vacancy form.
Send the ID of Director Mr. Vayid when required.
Check email regularly for EIC follow-up.
Screen local candidates with questions where needed before final EIC reporting.
Before sending the final EIC report, let the team verify it.
candidateDocuments: Check whether each expatriate already has a passport.
Collect passport-size photo / recent passport photo.
Check whether medical tests already exist or whether medical still needs to be arranged.
Create one folder per candidate and save passport, photo, medical, and related documents.
clearancesAndLap: Keep fire clearance and sanitary clearance moving in parallel.
Medical covering letter is deposited at Ebène by Mr. Vayid irrespective of expatriate case.
Fire clearance is handled in Port Louis.
Sanitary clearance depends on the region / health office.
Once medical, fire, and sanitary documents are ready, submit the LAP set and track status.
Pay LAP processing fee and then the applicable LAP issue fee at Victoria House, Port Louis.
If there is no existing lodging, continue housing search near the company while clearances are ongoing.
permitProcess: After clearances, begin contract / COE preparation and work permit processing.
For Madagascar only, the contract must be vetted / stamped.
For Nepal and India, no VET is required.
Submit work permit through EDB and track for queries and payment stages.
Once approved, download the work permit and send it to the team.
Liaise with PIO for entry permit and call to inquire whether it is ready.
Liaise with the agent for flight tickets so expatriates can travel to Mauritius.
After arrival, follow up with PIO for residence permit and related payment / validity checks.
